company.name: Galadyne
company.description: Develops liquid-propulsion missile systems designed for mass production, reviving a propulsion approach that eliminates reliance on fragile energetics supply chains. Addresses critical U.S. missile inventory gaps.
company.insight: Founded by Chandler Luzsicza, SpaceX's lead propulsion engineer on Starship before a stint at Saronic. Liquid propulsion is a contrarian bet that unlocks dramatically better unit economics than solid-fuel missiles at scale.

company.name: Starpath Robotics
company.description: In-situ resource utilization (ISRU) technology to produce rocket propellant from lunar ice. Building full-stack propellant production hardware for the Moon. Founded by former SpaceX engineers. Won NASA Break the Ice Challenge.
company.insight: If you can make fuel on the Moon, you fundamentally change the economics of deep space exploration. Starpath's ex-SpaceX team brings production engineering DNA to the most important unsolved problem in space logistics. NASA's Break the Ice Challenge win is credibility gold.

company.name: Viridian Space
company.description: Air-breathing electric propulsion for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellites. ASET technology scoops rarefied atmospheric oxygen at 200-300km altitude to fuel plasma thrusters, enabling indefinitely refuelable spacecraft.
company.insight: Viridian's air-breathing propulsion could be a game-changer for VLEO — satellites that never run out of fuel, with better imaging resolution and lower-latency comms than higher orbits. DoD interest is strong with two Phase II SBIRs and a five-year AFRL CRADA.

company.name: Nordic Air Defence
company.description: Kreuger 100 low-cost pulsed-propulsion drone interceptor (<$5k target), tested by Swedish Armed Forces and in Ukraine.
company.insight: Ukraine proved $500 drones overwhelm $500k defenses. Nordic Air Defence is building the mirror — $5k defenses that flip the curve back.

company.name: Scout AI
company.description: Vision-language-action foundation model ('Fury') for military robotics — $15M seed, G01 ground vehicle + A01 drone, Army UxS contract.
company.insight: VLAs are eating robotics. Scout AI is the defense-first bet with real Army contracts.

sbir_matches.topic: Autonomous Swarm Systems for Maritime Operations

company.name: Arondite
company.description: AI 'connective tissue' middleware linking disparate defense sensors, autonomous platforms, and robotics.
company.insight: Defense doesn't need more sensors; it needs them talking. Arondite's ex-Palantir / ex-Helsing team is building that translation layer.

company.name: Ark Robotics
company.description: Frontier OS — AI control layer unifying UGVs, drones, and USVs at 2,000 km range, demonstrated live at DALO Expo Denmark 2025.
company.insight: One operator controlling a fleet of cheap robots at range is the labor-arbitrage model for modern warfare. Ark built the OS first.

company.name: Alpine Eagle
company.description: Sentinel air-to-air counter-drone system with airborne radar and interceptor drones — Bundeswehr launch customer 2024, Ukrainian AF trials 2025.
company.insight: Ground-based C-UAS is saturated. Airborne C-UAS is the next wave, and Alpine Eagle has the Bundeswehr first customer.

company.name: HIMERA
company.description: EW-resistant 256-bit encrypted tactical radios with 4-day battery life, now supplying NATO end-users.
company.insight: Western tactical comms are overpriced museum pieces. HIMERA is retiring them with radios designed for modern EW conditions.

company.name: DroneForge
company.description: AI-powered hardware platform and SDK that transforms any commercial drone into a smart autonomous system for logistics, 3D modeling, and monitoring. Plug-and-play controller adds intelligence to existing airframes.
company.insight: Instead of building yet another drone, DroneForge makes existing drones smarter. The SDK approach means they can ride the hardware wave without the capital intensity. Very early stage but the platform play for drone autonomy is massive.

company.name: AQT
company.founder: Rainer Blatt, Thomas Monz, Peter Zoller
company.description: Trapped-ion quantum computers in 19-inch rack form factor — won €12.28M EU tender for trapped-ion system at LRZ Munich 2025.
company.insight: Rack-form quantum computers win the sovereign-AI-datacenter contest. AQT has the first EU tender.

name: Poseidon Aerospace

company.name: Poseidon Aerospace
company.description: Ground-effect vehicle company reviving ekranoplan technology with modern composites and autonomous systems. Building craft that fly feet above waves - faster than ships, cheaper than planes, independent of runways.
company.insight: Maritime autonomy is the defense frontier nobody is watching. Poseidon is building underwater autonomous systems at the intersection of naval warfare and commercial ocean infrastructure.

signals: 21 on Autonomous Swarm Systems for Maritime Op

sbir_matches.topic: Autonomous Swarm Systems for Maritime Operations

company.name: PILGRIM
company.description: Pioneering battlefield-ready biotech to revolutionize military medicine. Developing advanced medical countermeasures for warfighters.
company.insight: AI-first drug discovery platform focused on protein engineering. Jake Adler is building what Recursion should have been — leaner, faster, with better founder-market fit.

company.name: Exodys Energy
company.description: Recycling nuclear waste into fuel using molten salt fast reactor technology. Previously called Elysium Industries. Turning the nuclear waste 'problem' into a fuel source.
company.insight: Exodys tackles the nuclear waste narrative head-on by turning it into an asset. If they can prove waste-to-fuel economics, it removes one of nuclear's biggest political obstacles.

sbir_matches.topic: Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

company.name: QuantumDiamonds
company.description: Diamond NV-center quantum sensors for non-destructive semiconductor chip inspection — €152M facility announced December 2025.
company.insight: Semiconductor metrology + quantum sensing is a €100B unserved market. QuantumDiamonds has German sovereign backing.

company.name: Quobly
company.founder: Maud Vinet, Tristan Meunier, François Perruchot
company.description: Silicon-spin qubit quantum processors using CMOS fabrication — CEA-Leti spinout and PROQCIMA winner.
company.insight: Silicon-spin qubits inherit the world's most mature manufacturing supply chain. If they work, they win on cost by orders of magnitude.

company.name: Kreios Space
company.description: Very-Low-Earth-Orbit (VLEO) satellite platform with air-breathing propulsion for defense and EO missions.
company.insight: VLEO doubles resolution but demands propulsion no one has fielded. Kreios is NATO-backed and the leading contender.

company.name: Persona AI
company.description: Industrial humanoid robots for shipyard welding — partnership with HD Hyundai to deliver production prototype by 2026; ex-NASA Valkyrie and Figure CTO team.
company.insight: Shipyard welding is labor-starved globally. A humanoid that can do it wins the largest unserved industrial labor market.

company.name: Comand AI
company.description: AI decision platform ingesting battlefield data for commanders — €12M round September 2025.
company.insight: European militaries want a non-US commander AI. Comand is the cleanest bet.

company.name: Hyfix
company.description: Custom silicon for US-made autonomous drone systems — GPS-chip displacement of Chinese supply chain; $15M Craft Ventures-backed April 2026.
company.insight: Every US drone today contains Chinese chips. Hyfix is the first credible sovereign-silicon replacement.

company.name: Humanoid
company.description: HMND 01 dual-armed humanoid for warehouses, logistics, and retail — with NVIDIA partnership and live pilots. $50M in founder-led capital (not VC-raised).
company.insight: Europe needs a humanoid champion. Humanoid's 170-person team and retail/logistics pilots make it the realistic candidate.

company.name: Ouros Energy
company.description: Ultra-high-energy-density battery chemistry claiming 10x energy density at 1/100th the cost — early-stage R&D with $3M seed.

company.name: Stellaria
company.founder: Nicolas Breton (CEO); backed by CEA + Schneider Electric
company.description: Molten-salt Breed-and-Burn fast-neutron reactor — €23M June 2025 and France 2030 funding with Orano consortium.
company.insight: France has more spent fuel than any other country. Stellaria's reactor eats it — a politically unkillable design.

company.name: Bone AI
company.location: Palo Alto, CA / Seoul, South Korea
company.description: Autonomous UGV/USV/UAV platforms for defense reconnaissance and perimeter security — $12M Series A November 2025.
company.insight: Korea's primes are stagnant. Bone AI is a fresh-team challenger with Asian-market focus.

company.name: Fire Point
company.founder: Denys Shtilerman, Iryna Terekh, Yehor Skalyha
company.description: Long-range strike drones (FP-1, 1,600 km) and the FP-5 'Flamingo' cruise missile (3,000 km, 1,150 kg warhead).
company.insight: Cheap mass-produced cruise missiles reshape deterrence math. Fire Point is the only Western-aligned firm shipping them today.

name: EX-Fusion

company.name: EX-Fusion
company.description: Laser inertial-confinement fusion — Osaka University spinout demonstrating precision laser targeting; Series A ¥2.6B June 2025 plus JX Advanced Metals extension October 2025.
company.insight: NIF proved ignition. The next step is high-repetition-rate lasers — EX-Fusion has the Osaka University heritage and JX funding.

signals: 16 on Compact Fusion Pilot Plant Technologies

company.name: Hexana
company.description: Sodium-cooled SMR (twin 400 MWe blocks) with integrated thermal storage — €21M seed + France 2030 support.
company.insight: Fast-reactor + thermal storage = dispatchable firm zero-carbon power. Hexana is France's flagship bet on the pair.

company.name: Cascade Space
company.description: Building a turnkey deep space communications-as-a-service platform with ground station networks and software tools for lunar and deep space missions, replacing the oversubscribed NASA Deep Space Network.

company.name: SpectralX
company.description: Multispectral signature-management and concealment materials for troops and vehicles — IDF adoption and NATO orders.
company.insight: Modern sensors defeat WWII-era camouflage. SpectralX rebuilds stealth for the full EM spectrum.

company.name: Reflex Robotics
company.description: Wheeled humanoid mobile manipulator at $10k target price — $7M Khosla-led, team from Boston Dynamics Stretch and Tesla Model S line; hundreds of deployments planned 2025.
company.insight: Wheels > legs for most industrial use cases. Reflex is the cheapest credible humanoid.

company.name: Sunday Robotics
company.description: General-purpose home and industrial robot from OpenAI-adjacent founding team — raised significant seed in 2024-25 with NVIDIA research pedigree.
company.insight: Home is the hardest robot market. Sunday's OpenAI-adjacent team is one of the few betting on it.

company.name: Airship Industries
company.description: Next-generation blimp and airship technology for cargo transport, surveillance, and communication relay. Reimagining lighter-than-air vehicles.
company.insight: Lighter-than-air heavy cargo transport fills a gap between trucks, ships, and planes. If the economics work, airships are the most energy-efficient way to move heavy freight long distances.

company.name: Astrolight
company.description: Optical inter-satellite and ground-space laser communications terminals — selected for NATO DIANA.
company.insight: Optical links will replace RF for all serious space comms. Astrolight is the Baltic entrant in a three-horse race.

name: Scout Space

company.name: Scout Space
company.description: GEO space-domain-awareness optical sensors and on-board RSO data processing for Space Force Space Safari office — $10M+ equity plus multiple TACFI Phase II awards.
company.insight: Space Force needs on-orbit SSA, not just ground. Scout Space is the rare operator with Phase II contracts and hardware flying.

signals: 12 on Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

sbir_matches.topic: Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

company.name: Gauss Fusion
company.description: European stellarator fusion consortium — published Europe's first commercial fusion CDR in October 2025, with industrial partners Leonardo, RINA, and Siemens Energy. CEO Milena Roveda stepping down April 15, 2026.
company.insight: Europe's answer to Helion/CFS. Gauss's industrial partners (Leonardo, Siemens) give it realistic path to build.

company.name: Fractile
company.description: In-memory SRAM-based inference chip architecture aiming to dethrone Nvidia at LLM inference economics.
company.insight: If inference becomes 90% of AI compute, the chip optimized for it — not training-first GPUs — wins the biggest slice.

company.name: Q-Factor
company.founder: Nir Davidson, Ofer Firstenberg, Yoav Sagi, Guy Raz
company.description: Neutral-atom quantum computer targeting million-qubit scale — $24M seed April 2026 with Intel Capital.
company.insight: Neutral atoms are the consensus path to millions of qubits. Q-Factor has Intel Capital plus an elite team.

name: Pale Blue

company.name: Pale Blue
company.description: Water-based ion and resistojet thrusters for smallsats — lower toxicity than hydrazine with up to $27M in Japanese government grants.
company.insight: Hydrazine is becoming a regulatory headache. Water thrusters solve handling costs — Pale Blue is ahead of the curve.

signals: 10 on AI-Powered Cybersecurity for Critical In

company.name: Durin
company.description: Automated drill rigs for infrastructure and mining. Bringing autonomous precision drilling to construction, utilities, and resource extraction.
company.insight: Automating mining engineering and geology to unlock critical mineral supply. Ted Feldmann's team is solving the workforce bottleneck that's holding back US rare earth independence.

company.name: Axibo AI
company.description: Humanoid robots for manufacturing and logistics — C$11M funding round late 2025.
company.insight: Humanoid robotics still has room for national champions. Axibo is Canada's contender.

company.name: DIRAC
company.description: Anti-software software company that generates assembly instructions for industrial manufacturers. Solving broken work instruction creation processes in manufacturing with AI-generated visual guides.
company.insight: DIRAC is tackling one of manufacturing's most overlooked problems: getting the right instructions to workers on the floor. Bad work instructions cause defects, slowdowns, and training bottlenecks.

company.name: Picogrid
company.description: Unified data integration platform connecting sensors, end effectors, and autonomous systems. Multiple Air Force and Army contracts. Integrates with Skydio, Palantir, Northrop Grumman.
company.insight: Unified operating system for unmanned military vehicles and sensors. The software layer that connects the explosion of new defense hardware — potential platform play.

company.name: Mindgrove Technologies
company.description: Fabless RISC-V high-performance microcontrollers — IIT Madras spinout, first indigenous high-performance commercial MCU taped out 2025.
company.insight: India is investing $10B in semiconductors. Mindgrove is one of the first indigenous design houses with real silicon.

name: POLARIS Raumflugzeuge

company.name: POLARIS Raumflugzeuge
company.description: Reusable hypersonic spaceplane Aurora with aerospike engines — Bundeswehr BAAINBw contract 2025 for reusable hypersonic spaceplane development.
company.insight: Hypersonic spaceplanes remain a military monopoly. POLARIS is the first serious Western commercial attempt.

signals: 15 on Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal

sbir_matches.topic: Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal Protection

company.name: Deep Atomic
company.description: Zurich-based nuclear startup developing next-generation reactor technology. Pre-seed stage with ambitious plans for compact nuclear power systems.

company.name: FlyBy Robotics
company.description: Building low-cost drone swarms for military applications. Proving that quantity has a quality all its own with $2,000 drones vs $200,000 military alternatives.

company.name: Mimic Robotics
company.description: Robotic humanoid hands with sub-millimeter fingertip precision for manufacturing and logistics — $16M Series B 2025.
company.insight: Humanoids are bottlenecked on hands, not bodies. Mimic could be the Tier-1 hand supplier.

company.name: Greenroom Robotics
company.description: AI autonomous vessel software (GAMA / Lookout+) turning any boat into a USV — AUKUS Authorised User status granted 2025.
company.insight: AUKUS maritime autonomy needs autonomy software that installs on existing hulls. Greenroom is the approved vendor.

company.name: Swarm Aero
company.founder: Danny Goodman, Oliver Palmer, Peter Kalogiannis
company.description: Developer of unmanned aerial vehicles featuring sensor mesh networks, edge computing, and collaborative autonomy for large-scale drone operations in contested environments.

company.name: Aeon Industrial
company.description: Develops software-defined tactical weapon systems with vertically integrated manufacturing of propellant, rocket motors, fuzes, and flight computers. Flagship Zeus missile uses ODIN autonomous targeting software for automatic threat identification and persistent tracking.

company.name: DiamFab
company.description: Diamond-substrate power semiconductors for EV, renewables, nuclear, and quantum applications — CNRS spinout building a pilot line.
company.insight: Diamond is the endgame power semiconductor — nothing beats its thermal/electrical properties. DiamFab has the only scalable growth process.

company.name: RoboTwin
company.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák
company.description: Handheld 'teach-by-demonstration' device plus no-code software that turns factory workers into robot programmers — €2.3M EIC 2025, deployed in Central Europe, Netherlands, Mexico, Canada.
company.insight: Small factories can't hire robotics engineers. RoboTwin lets any worker program a robot in minutes.

company.name: SiFly
company.description: Long-endurance NDAA-compliant commercial and government drones (Q12, Q250) outperforming DJI on flight time and payload — $15M+ raised.
company.insight: US needs non-DJI drones yesterday. SiFly is the first that actually beats DJI on specs.

name: Trilobio

company.name: Trilobio
company.description: Fully automated robotic 'lab-in-a-box' platform for biologists, combining robotics, lab equipment, and no-code software to enable reproducible, rapid research in synthetic biology and life sciences.
company.insight: Addresses the reproducibility crisis in biology — 77% of biologists cannot reproduce their own results. When you automate the entire wet lab workflow, you solve reproducibility and 10x throughput simultaneously. Co-founders named to Forbes 30 Under 30.

signals: 15 on Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

sbir_matches.topic: Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

company.name: Solideon
company.description: Develops autonomous multi-robot micro-factories using Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing (WAAM) for 3D-printing large metallic aerospace and defense structures. Forward-deployable cells can print, post-process, assemble, and inspect without human intervention.

company.name: PierSight
company.description: Building the world's first combined SAR + AIS satellite constellation for maritime surveillance. Detects dark vessels, illegal fishing, and oil spills from space at 1/6th the cost of existing solutions.

company.name: Curio Solutions
company.description: Advanced nuclear fuel recycling (NuCycle) turning spent fuel into HALEU plus medical isotopes — $16M+ Series A plus DOE partnerships.
company.insight: The US has 90,000 metric tons of spent fuel. Curio's recycling is the politically palatable way to use it.

company.name: Kipu Quantum
company.founder: Enrique Solano, Daniel Volz, Tobias Grab
company.description: Hardware-agnostic application-specific quantum algorithms — achieved record protein-folding on IonQ in June 2025.
company.insight: Benchmarks matter — Kipu's protein-folding record on IonQ is one of the clearer quantum-utility demonstrations to date.

company.name: Canada Rocket Company
company.description: Small-lift orbital rocket by ex-SpaceX and CSA founders — $14.7M total and IDEaS defense grant.
company.insight: CRC's ex-SpaceX team is the pedigree NordSpace lacks. Two horses in Canada's sovereign launch race.

company.name: Magdrive
company.description: Solid-metal-propellant electric thrusters — first two launched to orbit in June 2025 — enabling eventual harvesting of space debris as fuel.
company.insight: Solid metal thrusters reframe orbital debris from liability to fuel stockpile — a narrative-shifting primitive.

name: Nuclearn.ai

company.name: Nuclearn.ai
company.description: AI platform for nuclear plant operations — used by 20+ US reactors; $10.5M Series A with utility industry adoption.
company.insight: US nuclear fleet needs operational AI. Nuclearn is at 20+ plants with little competition.

signals: 20 on Compact Fusion Pilot Plant Technologies

company.name: Emerald AI
company.description: Software flexing AI data-center load to stabilize grids — $24.5M seed NVIDIA-backed.
company.insight: AI data center demand is destabilizing grids. Emerald AI turns that demand into dispatchable flexibility.

name: Astro Mechanica

company.name: Astro Mechanica
company.description: Electric Adaptive Engine for supersonic flight that transitions from turbofan to ramjet. Engine separates compressor from traditional jet engine using electric motors, targeting subsonic to Mach 5 speeds. Planning affordable supersonic flights AND space launch systems - claims 3x cheaper payload delivery vs rockets.
company.insight: Variable-cycle adaptive turbofan engine could make sustained supersonic flight economically viable for the first time. Ian Brooke is one of the most underrated propulsion founders in the space.

signals: 18 on Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal

sbir_matches.topic: Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal Protection

company.name: Andrenam
company.description: AI-powered distributed maritime acoustic sensing for underwater and surface security. Ex-SpaceX team. $10M Seed from First Round Capital. Participated in Navy exercises.

company.name: Aurelius Systems
company.description: Laser-based point-defense systems for counter-drone and counter-missile applications. Redefining air defense with directed energy at unprecedented engagement ranges.

company.name: Herasight
company.description: Polygenic embryo screening company helping with embryo selection during IVF by revealing genetic variation. Served 80+ customers while in stealth mode. Making genetic insights accessible for family planning.
company.insight: Herasight is at the frontier of reproductive genomics — giving parents more information about genetic predispositions during IVF. The ethical debates are real, but demand is growing.

company.name: Sorcerer
company.description: Deploys a global network of persistent high-altitude weather balloons that remain airborne 6+ months each, completing ~30 laps around the globe while collecting 1000x more atmospheric data than existing systems for AI weather forecasting.

company.name: Duranium
company.description: Carbon-neutral production of titanium, magnesium, aluminum, zirconium, and hafnium via CO2-recycling carbochlorination. U.S. reshoring of critical metals. YC-backed.

company.name: Ephos
company.founder: Andrea Rocchetto, Francesco Ceccarelli, Ciro Pentangelo
company.description: Glass-substrate photonic chips for quantum computing and AI data centers — €41.5M EU Chips Act grant in July 2025.
company.insight: Photonic compute will dominate AI interconnect. Ephos builds the first glass-substrate fab — a category of its own.

company.name: Deepnight
company.description: Next-generation night vision and low-light imaging technology. Advanced sensors for military and defense situational awareness in darkness.

company.name: Rangeview
company.description: Automated foundry using robotics and materials science for investment casting of metal parts. Air Force SBIR for turbine components. Expanded El Segundo facility.
company.insight: Autonomous subsystems manufacturing for small satellites. The team from Varda is going after the component layer that space companies keep building in-house because nobody else can deliver it fast enough.

company.name: Theseus
company.founder: Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, Sacha Lévy
company.description: GPS-denied drone navigation technology. Enabling autonomous drone operations in contested electromagnetic environments where GPS is jammed or unavailable.

company.name: Mara
company.description: Autonomous AI-powered robotic defence systems designed to protect military assets and critical infrastructure from drone attacks.

company.name: Terranova
company.description: Terraforming robots that inject wood slurry underground for flood prevention — pilot in San Rafael with $7M seed.
company.insight: Geoengineering for climate resilience — a category the Overton window is finally opening up. Laurence Allen is building the regulatory and technical infrastructure years ahead of consensus.

company.name: Icarus
company.description: Defense drone company building next-generation autonomous aerial systems for military reconnaissance and strike missions.

company.name: Rivet Industries
company.description: Purpose-built tools combining hardware, software, and sensors for defense personnel and skilled industrial workers in harsh conditions. Awarded $195M contract.

company.name: Distributed Spectrum
company.founder: Alex Wulff, Ben Harpe, Isaac Struhl
company.description: Real-time RF sensing using AI for electronic warfare and situational awareness. Turning radio signals into actionable intelligence for military operations.

name: Anthrogen

company.name: Anthrogen
company.description: Uses CRISPR-engineered photosynthetic microbes and AI-designed enzymes to convert atmospheric CO2 into valuable chemicals and sustainable aviation fuel. Claims to have engineered the fastest-known photosynthesizing organism.

signals: 13 on Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

sbir_matches.topic: Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

name: Terrahaptix

company.name: Terrahaptix
company.description: AI-powered surveillance drones for critical infrastructure protection (dba Terra Industries) — $34M from 8VC + Lux Capital; won $1.2M NetHawk contract over an Israeli firm; exports to 8 African countries.
company.insight: Africa's best defense-tech story of 2025 — and it's backed by top US funds. Terra is the continent's first credible hard-tech defense unicorn trajectory.

sbir_matches.topic: Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal Protection

company.name: Epic Aerospace
company.description: Chimera orbital transfer vehicle (LEO→GEO delivery) — demo Chimera GEO-1 flew February 2025 on IM-2 but failed to transition orbit due to suspected comms failure. Y Combinator + Thiel Fellowship-backed.
company.insight: Orbital tugs are the picks-and-shovels of space. Epic brings LatAm engineering talent to the problem.

company.name: Wardstone
company.description: Space-based interceptors for missile defense — YC Fall 2025 with sovereign-space thesis.

company.name: Allen Control Systems
company.founder: Mike Wior, Luke Allen, Steven Simoni
company.description: Bullfrog autonomous gun turret achieves ~$10 cost-per-kill against drones. Bolts onto standard military weapons. Demonstrated swarm engagement capabilities at DoD events.
company.insight: The Bullfrog turret is a game-changer for counter-drone warfare. At $10 per kill vs $100K+ missiles, the economics are transformative. Series A led by Craft Ventures.

company.name: Modern Intelligence
company.founder: John Dulin, Joe Cieslik, Tristan Tager
company.description: AI company building proprietary advances in information and complexity theory for defense applications. Maritime surveillance AI product Cutlass provides real-time sensor fusion.

company.name: DefSecIntel
company.description: Mobile sensor-fusion and autonomous surveillance platforms (SurveilSPIRE) — lead integrator for the Estonian/Baltic Drone Wall.
company.insight: NATO's eastern flank needs a drone wall fast. DefSecIntel is the prime contractor that actually ships.

name: Senra Systems

company.name: Senra Systems
company.description: Software-defined manufacturing of complex wire harnesses. $25M Series A co-led by Figma CEO and CIV. Investors include Sequoia, a16z, Founders Fund.
company.insight: AI-powered wire harness manufacturing — a critical but invisible bottleneck for every complex machine from cars to spacecraft. The Torrance team is automating what's still largely manual work.

signals: 10 on AI-Powered Cybersecurity for Critical In

company.name: Tenna Systems
company.description: Software platform that transforms existing sensor data into real-time maps of the radio-frequency domain, protecting against jamming and spoofing.

company.name: Blitzpanel
company.description: Software-enabled factory for industrial control panels. Engineers design panels in a browser-based tool with instant quoting, and Blitzpanel automates manufacturing and shipping of UL-listed panels.

company.name: Dynamo Air
company.description: Planetary, on-demand autonomous heavy-lift system that moves civilization's heaviest loads with a click, anywhere on Earth. Team from SpaceX, Shield AI, Relativity, and Boeing Phantom Works.

company.name: Elementl Power
company.description: Nuclear project developer for utilities — $50M+ raised; announced Google 1.8 GW nuclear deal 2025.
company.insight: Hyperscalers need nuclear and can't build it themselves. Elementl is the first developer they picked.

name: Thalassa Robotics

company.name: Thalassa Robotics
company.founder: Vatsal Varma, Arjun Prabhakar, Bryan Tan
company.description: Advanced underwater robotics platform for defense and industrial maritime applications.

name: Delian Alliance Industries

company.name: Delian Alliance Industries
company.founder: Dimitrios Kottas, Georgios Kontogiannis, Ioannis Souriadakis
company.description: European defense tech company building autonomous maritime defense systems. Bridging Greek naval heritage with modern autonomous technology.

company.name: ParityQC
company.description: Parity Twine quantum-architecture IP for optimization problems — €12.4M BMBF consortium anchor.
company.insight: Parity architecture solves QUBO problems faster at scale. ParityQC is Austria's quiet quantum champion.

company.name: Hypernova Space Technologies
company.description: Metal-fueled plasma thrusters for satellites — novel physics with flight demo 2023 and Series A 2024.
company.insight: Metal-fueled plasma is a novel physics bet. Hypernova has the world's first commercial metal-fueled thruster.

company.name: Limelight Steel
company.description: Uses laser-powered furnaces to produce zero-emission iron from commercial ore grades, replacing coal-fired blast furnaces. Patented technology heats iron ore above 1,600°C using concentrated light instead of coal.

company.name: Overview Energy
company.description: Develops space-based solar power that beams energy from geosynchronous orbit via infrared lasers to existing utility-scale solar farms on Earth, enabling near-24/7 solar generation. Has demonstrated airborne laser-to-ground power transmission over 5 km.

company.name: Marathon Fusion
company.description: Develops critical fuel cycle technologies for fusion reactors, primarily high-throughput tritium processing systems using superpermeation technology. Signed LOIs with both Commonwealth Fusion Systems and Helion Energy.

company.name: Gallox Semiconductors
company.description: Gallium oxide (Ga2O3) ultra-wide-bandgap power semiconductors. Next-gen power devices beyond SiC and GaN. Cornell spinout.

company.name: Atmos Space Cargo
company.description: Inflatable reentry capsule 'Phoenix' — first flight April 2025; reusable in-orbit manufacturing downmass provider.
company.insight: Affordable downmass is the orbital-manufacturing keystone. Atmos's inflatable design is the German answer.

name: Thea Energy

company.name: Thea Energy
company.description: Planar-coil stellarator fusion using HTS magnets — $37M+ Series A plus $20M DOE milestone award; pivoting to Eos fusion pilot plant in mid-2020s.
company.insight: Stellarators beat tokamaks on stability. Thea's planar-coil approach could win on manufacturing cost.

signals: 17 on Compact Fusion Pilot Plant Technologies

name: Cache Energy

company.name: Cache Energy
company.description: Develops long-duration thermochemical energy storage using lime-based pellets that store renewable energy for 100+ hours at ambient conditions. Non-toxic, non-flammable technology that won DOE pitch competition at the White House.

signals: 12 on Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

sbir_matches.topic: Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

name: Asterra

company.name: Asterra
company.description: Satellite-based subsurface leak detection using SAR and L-band radar physics — $20M+ raised, deployed with ~1,000 utilities globally.
company.insight: Water infrastructure loses 30%+ to leaks globally. Asterra is the only satellite-data firm detecting them at continental scale.

signals: 13 on AI-Powered Cybersecurity for Critical In

company.name: AIR
company.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg
company.description: Two-seat consumer eVTOL and heavy-lift uncrewed cargo eVTOL — FAA MOSAIC certification September 2025, $1B+ orderbook.
company.insight: MOSAIC certification is a regulatory arbitrage. AIR got it first — $1B+ orders speak.

company.name: Vyoma
company.description: Space-domain-awareness constellation with optical sensors for collision avoidance — ESA contracts and European sovereign SSA positioning.
company.insight: Europe needs sovereign SSA as LEO fills up. Vyoma is the ESA-backed lead.

company.name: CX2 Industries
company.description: Advanced RF sensing systems for battlefield situational awareness. Vadris drone payload detects enemy drone pilots. Wraith drone provides electronic warfare capabilities. $31M Series A from a16z and Point72.
company.insight: Electronic warfare is the missing layer in modern defense. CX2 builds software-defined EW for contested spectrum environments — the kind of systems traditional primes can't iterate fast enough to build.

company.name: Gravitics
company.description: Designs and manufactures large-volume space station modules and orbital habitats for commercial and defense applications. Won $125M Axiom Space contract and $60M STRATFI from Space Force to develop orbital carrier platforms.

company.name: GalaxEye
company.description: World's first multi-sensor (SAR + optical) satellite — IIT Madras spinout with $10M Series A and 2025 maiden launch.
company.insight: SAR and optical on one sat is unique. If GalaxEye flies reliably, it's category-defining.

name: Realta Fusion

company.name: Realta Fusion
company.description: Mirror-machine fusion using HTS magnets — U. Wisconsin spinout, DOE milestone awardee; targeting industrial-heat fusion first.
company.insight: Industrial heat is a lower bar than grid electricity. Realta targets it first, betting mirror fusion gets there cheaper.

signals: 18 on Compact Fusion Pilot Plant Technologies

company.name: The Nuclear Company
company.founder: Jonathan Webb, Patrick Maloney, Kiran Bhatraju
company.description: Uses proven reactor designs (AP1000) rather than inventing new reactors. Takes a developer/orchestrator approach similar to Brookfield for nuclear projects. Fleet-scale execution strategy targeting 6 GW in first fleet.
company.insight: The Nuclear Company's thesis is that reactor technology isn't the bottleneck — execution is. By using proven AP1000 designs and taking a developer approach, they can deploy faster than anyone designing new reactors.

company.name: Thoron
company.description: American drone manufacturer positioned as the alternative to China's DJI. Building the domestic commercial drone supply chain.

company.name: OBSIDIA Semiconductor
company.description: Semiconductor supply chain security company developing RF-based silicon verification technology to eliminate fraud and counterfeiting at the chip level. Their ReelScan product provides trust and provenance for semiconductor components.

company.name: Photonium
company.description: AI tools for optics and photonics design and EDA. Accelerating photonic chip and system design cycles.

company.name: Nu Quantum
company.description: Quantum networking hardware and interconnects for scalable quantum data centers, with National Grid leading its $60M Series A.
company.insight: Utility-scale quantum will be built from many connected QPUs. Nu Quantum is the clearest pure-play on that interconnect bottleneck.

company.name: Hexium
company.description: Atomic Vapor Laser Isotope Separation (AVLIS) for lithium isotope enrichment. Clean, scalable approach to nuclear fuel production using laser technology.

company.name: Cambridge Aerospace
company.description: Skyhammer jet-powered drone/missile interceptor — won UK MoD contract April 2026 with first deliveries in May.
company.insight: Counter-drone economics are broken. Cambridge Aerospace is one of very few solutions that cost less than the threats they kill.

company.name: Deterrence
company.founder: Dhruva Rajendra, Brian Jones, Henry Olgers
company.description: Automating munitions production at scale. Software-defined manufacturing for defense supply chain acceleration.

company.name: Space Machines Company
company.description: Optimus Orbital Servicing Vehicle — flew inaugural mission 2024; pursuing India-Australia space bridge with AU$8.5M Series A.
company.insight: In-orbit servicing is the next big space market. Space Machines flew Optimus — most peers are still pitching.

company.name: Nox Metals
company.description: AI-powered automated metals supply and processing. Custom-cut aluminum blocks for CNC machining delivered in seconds vs. days. YC S25. $100M+ revenue target.

company.name: KrattWorks
company.description: AI-enabled GPS-denied reconnaissance and strike drones — revenue scaled 26x to €19.7M in 2025.
company.insight: Russia showed GPS jamming is existential for Western drones. KrattWorks built the GPS-free fallback and has the real combat data.

company.name: KP Labs
company.description: Onboard AI computers and neural-network processors for satellites — Intuition-1 hyperspectral mission operational in orbit.
company.insight: Bandwidth to orbit is saturating. On-satellite AI is the only way to scale EO fleets — KP Labs has flight heritage.

name: Hypersonix Launch Systems

company.name: Hypersonix Launch Systems
company.description: 3D-printed hydrogen-fueled scramjet engines (DART AE, Mach 7) — DART AE flew successfully February 27, 2026 on Rocket Lab HASTE from Wallops (first Australian hypersonic flight test). $46M Series A with Saab, DIU HyCAT contract, UK MoD hypersonic missile contract.
company.insight: AUKUS is starved of hypersonic suppliers. Hypersonix is the rare non-US/non-Chinese option with real flight hardware.

signals: 20 on Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal

sbir_matches.topic: Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal Protection

company.name: Zeromark
company.description: AI-powered auto-aiming counter-drone systems for infantry. Giving ground soldiers the ability to detect and shoot down aerial threats in real time.

company.name: Portal Space Systems
company.description: Rapidly maneuverable Supernova spacecraft with solar-thermal propulsion delivering 6 km/s delta-V — $70M+ total including $50M Series A late 2025 and $45M STRATFI.
company.insight: Space Force wants RPO-capable spacecraft with huge delta-V. Portal's solar-thermal bet delivers where electric propulsion can't.

company.name: Orangewood Labs
company.founder: Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, Akash Bansal
company.description: Affordable, AI-powered collaborative robotic arms for small and medium businesses. Uses natural language programming (RoboGPT) to democratize industrial robotics at a fraction of traditional costs.
company.insight: The 'Tesla of robotic arms' play — making automation affordable enough for the 98% of factories that can't afford a $100K Universal Robots setup. Natural language programming via RoboGPT is a genuinely novel interface. Y Combinator backed. Crossed $1M ARR.

company.name: HLabs
company.description: Building a domestic U.S. supply chain for robotics actuators and electronics. Onshoring critical robotics components.

company.name: Corvex Systems
company.description: Domestically sourced drone motor manufacturing at scale. Building the U.S. supply chain for UAV propulsion systems.

company.name: Manastu Space
company.description: Green satellite propulsion using H2O2 (hydrogen peroxide) — flight-proven on PSLV 2024, IN-SPACe support.
company.insight: Hydrazine is getting regulated. Manastu's H2O2 thrusters are flight-proven and production-ready.

company.name: QuamCore
company.description: Superconducting-qubit fault-tolerant architecture targeting million-qubit systems — $26M Series A 2025.
company.insight: Fault tolerance is the endgame. QuamCore's architecture could leapfrog incumbents if the physics works.

company.name: Mbodi AI
company.description: Natural-language robot teaching platform. Program robots by telling them what to do instead of coding. New York-based.

company.name: Qedma
company.founder: Asif Sinay, Netanel Lindner, Dorit Aharonov
company.description: Software that suppresses quantum noise — $26M Series A led by Glilot+ with IBM and Korea Investment Partners.
company.insight: Before full fault tolerance, noise-suppression is the bridge. Qedma is IBM's partner of choice.

company.name: Corintis
company.founder: Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli
company.description: Silicon-based microfluidic chip cooling extracting 10x more heat than conventional methods — $58M Series A with Microsoft endorsement.
company.insight: AI chips are now thermally constrained, not transistor-constrained. Corintis is the clearest Microsoft-endorsed fix.

company.name: C12 Quantum Electronics
company.description: Carbon-nanotube spin-qubit quantum processors — ENS Paris spinout and PROQCIMA winner.
company.insight: Carbon nanotubes offer near-perfect qubits if fabrication can scale. C12 owns the science from Ecole Normale Supérieure.

company.name: Ultra Robotics
company.description: General-purpose factory robots. YC-backed. Building affordable, capable industrial robots for American manufacturing.

company.name: Axial Composites
company.description: Non-planar carbon fiber and thermoplastic composite manufacturing at software speed. Automated fiber-injection cells produce stronger, lighter parts in days vs. weeks. YC S25.

company.name: Krevera
company.description: AI for real-time closed-loop control and optimization of injection molding. Reducing defects and waste in plastics manufacturing.

company.name: Drafter
company.description: AI tools for manufacturing engineering. Automating production planning, process documentation, and factory floor intelligence.

company.name: Zettascale
company.description: Building energy-efficient AI chips designed for massive-scale computing. Next-generation chip architecture optimized for AI workloads.

company.name: Nascent Materials
company.description: Domestic LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate) cathode production. Building U.S. battery materials supply chain independence.

company.name: Radian Forge
company.description: Wire-arc additive manufacturing (WAAM) at large scale. Producing massive metal structures through robotic welding-based 3D printing.

company.name: Fossa Systems
company.description: 80+ picosatellites manufactured for IoT and defense downlink constellation — contracts with Spanish MoD and NATO.
company.insight: IoT-from-space needs cheap constellations. Fossa has shipped 80+ sats — rare real traction.

company.name: Clippership
company.founder: Nico Cymbalist, Luca Cymbalist, Kai Matsuka
company.description: Autonomous wind-powered cargo vessels. Reinventing zero-emission ocean freight using AI-controlled sailing ships for sustainable global trade.

company.name: Aalo Atomics
company.description: Pragmatic approach using off-the-shelf fuel and existing supply chains. Building test reactor in Idaho. Selected for DOE Reactor Pilot Program. Targeting July 4, 2026 activation.
company.insight: Modular microreactor purpose-built for AI data centers. The market for behind-the-meter nuclear is about to explode, and Aalo's XMR design is positioned to win it.

name: Amidon Heavy Industries

company.name: Amidon Heavy Industries
company.description: Autonomous underwater vehicles for defense and commercial applications. Building the next generation of unmanned undersea systems.

signals: 15 on Autonomous Swarm Systems for Maritime Op

sbir_matches.topic: Autonomous Swarm Systems for Maritime Operations

company.name: Long Wall
company.description: Space launch developer — entity formed 2017 as ABL Space Systems, rebranded Long Wall February 2025 with pivot to solid-fuel missile programs. Dan Piemont is CEO.

company.name: Wyvern
company.founder: Chris Robson, Callie Lissinna, Kurtis Broda, Kristen Cote
company.description: Commercial hyperspectral EO satellite constellation (Dragonette) — 4 satellites on orbit, selected by CSA for next-gen EO study.
company.insight: Hyperspectral EO is finally proving commercial ROI. Wyvern is the Canadian pure-play.

company.name: Layup Parts
company.description: On-demand composite parts and tooling with no minimums. Tech-enabled speed and cost for carbon fiber, fiberglass, and Kevlar components.

company.name: ADASI
company.description: UAE's national UAV manufacturer — launched Jeniah stealth UCAV and VORTEX-E counter-drone at UMEX 2026.
company.insight: Gulf defense wants sovereign drones. ADASI's Jeniah is the first indigenous stealth UCAV outside the superpowers.

company.name: General Matter
company.description: Rebuilding American uranium enrichment capacity for next-gen nuclear fuels. Founders Fund-incubated. $1.5B enrichment facility in Paducah, KY. $900M DOE HALEU contract.

company.name: Frankenburg Technologies
company.description: Estonian defense startup developing air-defense missile systems — €30M Series A in 2025 on top of earlier €10M. Led by CEO Kusti Salm (former Permanent Secretary of Estonia's MoD).
company.insight: One of the fastest-growing defense startups in Europe. Backed by NATO Innovation Fund and Plural. Significant interest from Gulf states. Addressing the critical air defense gap exposed by Ukraine.

company.name: HEO
company.description: Non-Earth imaging and satellite inspection — $12M Series A with contracts from US Space Force and UK MoD.
company.insight: Space inspection is going to explode as LEO fills up. HEO is already serving SPACECOM and UK MoD.

company.name: NetworkOcean
company.description: Underwater data center operator prototyping thermally efficient subsea compute — early pilot stage with SF Bay testing.

company.name: EvolutionaryScale
company.founder: Alexander Rives, Tom Sercu, Salvatore Candido
company.description: Built ESM3, the largest protein language model (98B params trained on 2.78B proteins) — generated novel esmGFP equivalent to 500M years of evolution. Science-published. $142M raised.
company.insight: Protein design will transform biology. EvolutionaryScale's ex-Meta FAIR team built the dominant protein LM.

company.name: Netrasemi
company.description: Fabless SoC design for CCTV/smart vision and IoT — ₹107 Cr Series A 2025, DLI-backed with test chips taped out.
company.insight: Vision chips are the second-largest semi market after compute. Netrasemi is India's shot at a native Ambarella.

company.name: Anyon Systems
company.description: Superconducting quantum computers with thousands of qubits and built-in error correction — selected as Canadian Quantum Champion.
company.insight: Canada is putting C$334M into domestic quantum. Anyon is one of four national champions.

name: Epoch Biodesign

company.name: Epoch Biodesign
company.description: Engineered enzymes that break down mixed plastic waste into chemical feedstocks — Inditex and lululemon are customers.
company.insight: Epoch's edge: brands (Inditex, lululemon) are co-investors AND customers. That flywheel is how you scale a plastic alternative.

signals: 13 on Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

sbir_matches.topic: Synthetic Biology for Space Life Support

company.name: Sagar Defence Engineering
company.description: Autonomous surface and underwater vessels — $25.4M Series A, DRDO partner under Navy iDEX DISC 7.
company.insight: India's Navy wants sovereign autonomy. Sagar is the lead startup under iDEX DISC 7.

company.name: Endurance Energy
company.description: Seafloor geothermal energy extraction. Tapping into vast thermal resources beneath the ocean floor for baseload clean power generation.

company.name: Vertical Semiconductor
company.founder: Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, Tomas Palacios
company.description: Vertical GaN power devices for efficient power conversion. Cambridge, MA-based semiconductor innovator.

company.name: Cohesive Robotics
company.description: AI-powered high-mix manufacturing workcells. Robots that adapt to part variability without reprogramming. Brooklyn-based.

company.name: GenFab Co
company.description: Building self-reproducing manufacturing systems — the minimum viable von Neumann constructor. Manufacturing cells installed in factory arrays that can fabricate precision electronic assemblies on demand.

company.name: RMFG
company.description: Fast U.S. custom sheet-metal parts, weldments, and assemblies. Software-defined contract manufacturing for hardware startups.

company.name: FlexAI
company.description: Universal AI compute orchestration across heterogeneous GPUs — €28.5M seed to scale training cloud.
company.insight: Every cloud provider wants to avoid Nvidia monopoly pricing. FlexAI's abstraction layer is the neutral gateway.

company.name: NordSpace
company.description: Canadian orbital launch vehicle and spaceport operator — $38M+ with maiden suborbital launch planned winter 2026.
company.insight: Canada has Kepler, MDA, and Telesat but no sovereign launch. NordSpace fills that gap with both rocket and pad.

company.name: Longshot Space
company.description: Building space launch cannons — a gun powered by pressurized air that can shoot payloads into orbit. Revolutionary non-rocket launch approach.

company.name: Morpheus Space
company.description: Scalable electric (FEEP) propulsion systems (GO-2) for smallsats — $15M Series A+ 2026, GO-2 demonstrated in orbit.
company.insight: Smallsat electric propulsion is a duopoly. Morpheus is the rising German entrant.

company.name: Vuecason
company.description: Wire-based metal 3D printing via nozzle deposition plus integrated CNC finishing. Making metal parts as easily as printing plastic.

company.name: Stark Defence
company.founder: Florian Seibel, Uwe Horstmann, Johannes Schaback
company.description: Virtus VTOL loitering munitions and AI C2 software — $62M Sequoia round and Bundeswehr framework contract up to €2.86B.
company.insight: Europe's defense wave needs German champions. Stark + €2.86B Bundeswehr deal is one.

company.name: Mission Zero Technologies
company.founder: Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, Gaël Gobaille-Shaw
company.description: Electrochemical direct air capture consuming less energy than thermal DAC, backed by Breakthrough Energy Ventures.
company.insight: DAC wins only if energy cost collapses. Electrochemistry is the most credible path and Mission Zero is its clearest purist.

company.name: SatRev
company.description: REC nanosatellite constellation targeting 1,500 real-time EO satellites by 2026, with mass-production facility under construction.
company.insight: If SatRev hits 1,500 real-time EO sats, Earth becomes the most-photographed object in history — and Poland owns the feed.

company.name: Nord Quantique
company.description: Developing hardware-efficient quantum error correction using bosonic qubit architectures. Enables longer coherence times and scalable logical qubit designs.

company.name: Theion
company.description: Crystal-sulfur cathode lithium batteries targeting 3x energy density — €25M+ raised.
company.insight: Li-S cells promise game-changing density if stability gets solved. Theion's crystal-sulfur approach has the cleanest path.

name: Stratolaunch

company.name: Stratolaunch
company.description: Reusable hypersonic testbed — two successful Talon-A2 flights exceeding Mach 5 December 2024 and March 2025; multi-flight MACH-TB DoD contract.
company.insight: DoD hypersonic programs need testbeds. Talon-A2 is the only one flying — monopoly asset.

signals: 14 on Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal

sbir_matches.topic: Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal Protection

company.name: Rhoman Aerospace
company.description: PX4 flight-controls software and firmware for DoD drone programs — SBIR Phase I/II awards with defense customers.

company.name: Sarla Aviation
company.description: Electric VTOL air taxis for Indian urban mobility — ₹1,300 Cr Sky Factory for 1,000 aircraft/year by 2029.
company.insight: India's eVTOL thesis works at Indian prices, not US prices. Sarla is the local champion scaling a 500-acre factory.

company.name: Occam
company.description: Developing the world's smallest brain-sensing wearable — a behind-the-ear nanosensor using dry EEG technology to track mental clarity, stress, and cognitive performance via non-invasive brain-computer interface.

company.name: ReOrbit
company.description: Software-defined sovereign satellites with inter-satellite laser links — €45M Series A was Europe's record space round of 2025.
company.insight: Every European government wants its own resilient LEO constellation. ReOrbit is positioned as the default supplier.

company.name: Reflex Aerospace
company.description: Custom-built defense-grade ISR satellites on Praetora platform — €50M Series A in November 2025 was largest European New Space round.
company.insight: Europe's record €50M space round validates the thesis: sovereign defense-grade satellites are the new demand driver.

company.name: Helical Fusion
company.description: Stellarator fusion using HTS coils — Series A $15.6M (July 2025) plus $5.5M extension (Dec 2025); signed Japan's first fusion PPA with Aoki Super.
company.insight: Stellarators beat tokamaks on stability but lose on magnet cost. HTS fixes that — Helical is Japan's flagship bet.

company.name: General Galactic
company.description: Creating sustainable hydrocarbon fuels - fossil-free natural gas for Earth and future Mars fuel stations. Turning captured CO2 into usable fuels.

company.name: Cellcolabs
company.description: Industrial-scale production of clinical-grade mesenchymal stem cells at Karolinska — $25M+ raised.
company.insight: Cell therapy is bottlenecked on manufacturing. Cellcolabs is the Karolinska-backed scale-up candidate.

company.name: MaverickX
company.description: Critical minerals recovery and refining (rebranded from Maverick Metals January 2026) — $19M seed round.

company.name: Brilliant Labs
company.description: Open-source AR glasses (Frame) — consumer frontier hardware with developer ecosystem; true consumer frontier hardware at affordable price point.
company.insight: AR glasses will happen. Brilliant Labs bets the winning platform will be open, not Apple's.

company.name: Rune Technologies
company.description: TyrOS - an AI operating system for military logistics, asset tracking, and personnel management. Optimizing the supply chain backbone of defense operations.

name: Dream Security

company.name: Dream Security
company.description: AI-driven cybersecurity for critical infrastructure — $100M+ Series B at $1.5B valuation November 2024 led by Bain Capital Ventures.
company.insight: Critical infrastructure is nation-state priority everywhere. Dream's NSO-alumni team moves faster than any incumbent.

signals: 14 on AI-Powered Cybersecurity for Critical In

company.name: GHGSat
company.description: Operates the world's largest fleet of high-resolution satellites dedicated to methane monitoring. Serves ExxonMobil, Aramco, and UN organizations.
company.insight: GHGSat is becoming the de facto global standard for methane emissions verification. As carbon markets mature, their data becomes critical infrastructure.

company.name: Lumina Vehicles
company.description: Electric autonomous heavy-duty construction equipment (Moonlander ML6 autonomous bulldozer) — ~$8M raised, Series A underway.

company.name: Grain Weevil
company.description: Agricultural robots that autonomously manage grain storage bins. Small grain-walking robots monitor temperature, break crusts, and level grain — replacing dangerous manual labor.

company.name: Observable Space
company.description: Ground-based telescope systems and space optics for space domain awareness and laser communications. Serves NASA, Space Force, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman.

company.name: Atom Bodies
company.founder: Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, Eric Monsef
company.description: AI-powered bionic limbs for amputees — YC-backed early-stage company with advanced prosthetics R&D.
company.insight: Biotech for human performance and longevity — programmable cellular therapies. The team from Bicycle and Denali brings deep experience from the best drug discovery platforms.

company.name: Artimus Robotics
company.description: Soft electrohydraulic artificial muscles (HASEL actuators). Fluidic, compliant actuators for next-gen robotics with human-like movement.

company.name: Signaloid
company.description: Builds uncertainty-tracking processors that natively propagate statistical uncertainty through computations, eliminating the need for Monte Carlo simulations and enabling faster probabilistic computing.

company.name: Diode
company.description: Revolutionizing circuit board design with AI automation. Reducing PCB development timelines from months to days. Accelerating hardware development cycles.

company.name: OpenStar
company.description: Developing a levitated dipole fusion reactor — the only company in the world pursuing this approach commercially. Achieved plasma confinement at 1M+ degrees with a levitated superconducting magnet.

company.name: Majestic Labs
company.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders
company.description: Disaggregated-memory AI server architecture using custom accelerator + memory interface chips — $100M+ raised.
company.insight: Memory is the new AI bottleneck. Majestic's ex-Google/Meta team is building the architectural fix.

company.name: Terraform Industries
company.description: Modular synthetic fuel production from solar energy and air. Terraformer converts sunlight + CO2 into pipeline-grade methane. Demonstrated 99.4% purity. $26M raised.
company.insight: Casey Handmer's bet that synthetic natural gas from solar + atmospheric CO2 can be cheaper than fossil fuels. If he's right, this is the single most important climate technology in existence.

company.name: Proteus Space
company.description: Building advanced space propulsion systems and orbital maneuvering vehicles for satellite servicing and space logistics.

company.name: Blackshark.ai
company.description: AI geospatial intelligence — processes 1.4M sq km/day of battlefield data into real-time 3D reconstructions for military planning.
company.insight: Selected for first AWS European Defence Accelerator. Backed by Microsoft M12 and Maxar. Processing scale is staggering — 1.4M sq km per day.

company.name: SemiQon
company.description: Building cryogenic CMOS transistors that consume 0.1% of the power of room-temperature equivalents, enabling scalable quantum computing control electronics. Positioning as Europe's first cryogenic semiconductor foundry.

company.name: Fergani Space
company.description: Turkey's private satellite and launch-vehicle developer (Uluğ Bey GPS constellation) — FGN-100-d1/d2 launched 2025.
company.insight: Turkey wants sovereign PNT. Fergani + Somalia spaceport is the most ambitious middle-power space play in the world.

name: constellr

company.name: constellr
company.description: Thermal-IR microsatellite constellation (HiVE) for defense-grade thermal intelligence — €37M Series A 2026.
company.insight: Thermal IR is a massive unserved signals market. constellr's defense angle separates it from OroraTech's civil focus.

signals: 17 on Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal

sbir_matches.topic: Hypersonic Vehicle Materials and Thermal Protection

company.name: Darkhive
company.description: Autonomous micro-drone swarm technology for indoor and GPS-denied environments. Tiny drones that can map and navigate buildings autonomously.
